tian_qi_alan_zhang.name: Tian Qi Alan Zhang
tian_qi_alan_zhang.summary:
Representing California, Tian Qi Alan Zhang has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U19 BD, U19 BS, Mixed Doubles, U19 XD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 10 career titles across 22 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Tian is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Tian is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, 5 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U19 BD, U19 BS, Mixed Doubles, U19 XD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (BD U15) • 3 ORC titles.

Tian has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 15 different players across the career:
  • Youshi Chen (current primary partner) — 6 events together this season alone with 4 titles, building serious momentum (6 total events in BD/U19, across 2 seasons)
  • Audrey Chang (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (5 total events in Mixed/U19/XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Matthew Lei — a trusted BD/U17 partner (6 events, best: #3, across 2 seasons)
  • Ella Mak — 3 events in U17/XD, best: #4, spanning 3 seasons

The 2024-2025 season has been a step up, with 5 titles across 6 tournaments — a clear sign that Tian is still ascending.

Tian has been on this journey through U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Tian has competed across 4 different regions (NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 7 seeded entries across the career, Tian consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 10 titles, Tian has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.
